description: Private-tier post. Audience-gated via the required audienceList reference; never federated; visible only to requesters in the audience list. The presence of audienceList is what structurally distinguishes private from public.

encodedContent.description: Optional codec output for at-rest content transform. When present, replaces the `text` field (XOR — exactly one of the two MUST be present, substrate-enforced per validate_record). Codec interpretation is opaque to the substrate; for laquna, this includes the 32-byte slug inline at the tail. See encodedContentCodec for the codec identifier.

encodedContentCodec.description: Operator-namespaced codec identifier (e.g. 'laquna/0.2') for the codec that produced `encodedContent`. Required at application layer when `encodedContent` is present (substrate-enforced per validate_record). Matches the Rust-side CodecId(BoundedString<128>) bound.

encodedContentGeneration.description: Optional per-record rotation generation mark. Stamped by the substrate from the resolved RotationOracle hint at encode time. Host rewrite-on-rotate jobs select records by `WHERE encodedContentGeneration < current_generation`. Matches the Rust-side RotationGenerationMark(BoundedString<128>) bound. Hosts MUST pick a lex-sortable encoding.

audienceList.description: Required AT-URI reference to the audience list governing visibility. Consulted at read time (not at post time); membership changes on the referenced audience apply retroactively to records carrying this reference.

publicCompanion.description: Optional AT-URI pointing at a paired public-tier record (the public side of a dual-faced post). The linkage lives only on the kryphocron-side record; the public-side record carries no kryphocron-visible markers. When absent, the post is a standalone private post with no public companion.
